title: [CI:DOCS] Fix manpage header formatting
body:
<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

In case you're only changing docs, make sure to prefix the pull-request title with "[CI:DOCS]". That will prevent functional tests from running and save time and energy.

Finally, be sure to sign commits with your real name. Since by opening
a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with
something like `git commit -s --amend`.
-->

Current podman manpages cause the Gnome yelp manpage viewer to fail to render the manpage with an error `Don't understand title line: 'tpodman(1)()'`. This is caused by the title line being malformed. Ultimately the problem is that go-md2man wants the title line to be space separated, not formatted with parenthesis around the section number.

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

<!--
If no, just write `None` in the release-note block below. If yes, a release note
is required: Enter your extended release note in the block below. If the PR
requires additional action from users switching to the new release, include the
string "action required".

For more information on release notes, please follow the Kubernetes model:
https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/release-notes.md
-->

```release-note
None
```

comments.body: LGTM from my end, but I'd like @edsantiago to take a look before we merge

How do the man pages render this?  Does it still come through as "podman-run(1)" for the title of the `podman run` page for instance?  All of our manpages reference using a number in parens, I'm not sure I want that to go away.  Does Gnome understand the parens if there's a space in between?  i.e. `podman-run (1)` vs the current `podman-run(1)`

I don't exactly understand the troff at play here, but the current man pages have
```
.nh
.TH podman(1)
.SH NAME
.PP
podman - Simple management tool for pods, containers and images
```
This renders as
```
podman(1)()                                                           podman(1)()

NAME
       podman - Simple management tool for pods, containers and images
```

After my patch, the troff is
```
.nh
.TH podman 1
.SH NAME
.PP
podman - Simple management tool for pods, containers and images
```
This renders as
```
podman(1)                                     General Commands Manual                                    podman(1)

NAME
       podman - Simple management tool for pods, containers and images
```

In the original case, `man` is not able to identify the section, so the "General Commands Manual" title of section 1 is also missing.
You are missing all of the man pages that are generated from podman/docs/source/markdown*md.in

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *baude*, *ventifus* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [baude] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

Changing the source files would also change how it looks on docs.podman.io for example. Should this be changed when the man pages are generated like we do already here: https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/8882b7664d225bf7f8efac6ab10e8d583e48c712/Makefile#L431

Wow... I'm amazed this has been present so long. There is no dispute whatsoever: this PR corrects a longstanding bug. (As in, the generated .1 files were broken and are now correct). As to whether it should be solved via `sed` hackery or by fixing the sources themselves, I'm inclined to think that the PR is correct: if `go-md2man` generates correct output given this input fix, then this is the correct fix.

@ventifus , please see @rhatdan's comment re: `*.md.in` files: fix those, too, and this should be all set to go. THANK YOU!
LGTM, and `grep '^%.*(' docs/source/markdown/*` finds no more hits. Others may ask you to squash your commits, I'll step back from that battle and will let others approve or not.

@edsantiago  Sounds good, thank you. The two commits don't really add anything to the history so I'll go ahead and squash. 

@Luap99 I'm trying to track down how the website docs are generated from the markdown to see what impact this change will have there, I'm not sure yet. But I can say that the sed you referred to should be fine, I've tested `make docs` output and it looks like how I expect.

I think @Luap99's question was whether to fix each individual file (as you did) or to do midstream surgery via `sed`. The latter would've been a smaller PR, but I think your approach is the correct one: this is a bug in our md files, and that is the place to fix it.

/lgtm
/hold
